methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $30M total raised proxy.

private.name: Daylight Computer
private.rationale: Same sector (Consumer Tech), same stage (Series A)

private.name: Whisper Aero
private.rationale: Same sector (Consumer Tech), same stage (Series A)

data.narrative:
Shinkei is a Series A consumer tech company founded by Saif Khawaja and Reed Ginsberg that has raised $30M to date. The comp universe consists of two private peers—Daylight Computer and Whisper Aero—with no direct public market comparables available in the consumer tech frontier segment.

The valuation analysis relies on stage-progression methodology, which applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A-to-subsequent-stage advancement to the $30M total raised as a proxy for current post-money valuation. This method yields an implied value of $60M. Given the limited methodological toolkit and single comp-based approach, triangulation yields a range anchored on this result: $51M on the low end, $60M at median, and $69M at the high end.

The narrow range reflects the constraint of having only stage-progression applicable; absence of revenue multiples, ARR comparables, or public market anchors limits conviction. Key uncertainties include unit economics validation, path to Series B fundraising readiness, and relative performance versus the two identified private peers. A more robust analysis would benefit from revenue-based multiples or additional private comp data to narrow the $18M spread between low and high estimates.
